name: Corn chip (food medium)
basis: per 100 g as consumed (dry corn/extruded corn chip)

components.name: starch
components.source: USDA FDC 168127 (Snacks, plain, chips, extruded, corn-based) = 14.38 g/28.35 g
components.evidence: DOMINANT carbon/energy substrate. A microbe grows on corn chips primarily by amylolysis of starch, not on free sugars or organic acids.

components.name: dietary fiber (corn arabinoxylan/bran)
components.evidence: Insoluble corn cell-wall polysaccharide; slowly fermentable.

components.name: sucrose
components.evidence: Only measurable free sugar; total sugars 0.28 g/100 g.

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: Below detection in the finished chip (starch is bound).

components.name: phytic acid (myo-inositol hexakisphosphate)
components.source: Raw maize ~1 g/100g; nixtamalization removes ~25-35% (Urizar & Bressani 1997, reviewed in Int J Food Sci 2025 doi:10.1155/ijfo/6961009)
components.evidence: Principal genuine organic acid of a corn chip and a phosphate/mineral chelator. Range 0.6-0.75 g/100 g; estimate, not a direct assay of this product.

components.name: total protein (maize/zein)
components.evidence: Corn (zein/glutelin) protein; profile below is the maize amino-acid pattern scaled to this protein content.

components.name: L-glutamate
components.source: USDA FDC 168867 cornmeal profile scaled to 6.10 g protein
components.evidence: Dominant amino acid of maize protein (zein-rich). Proxy from degermed yellow cornmeal, factor 0.547.

components.name: L-phenylalanine
components.evidence: MISSING from current record; added. Proxy.

components.name: L-serine
components.evidence: L-serine (current record listed D-serine, a non-biological isomer). Proxy.

components.name: L-valine
components.evidence: MISSING from current record; added. Proxy.

components.name: L-lysine
components.evidence: First-limiting amino acid of maize (low). Proxy.

components.name: L-tryptophan
components.evidence: Second-limiting amino acid of maize. Proxy.

components.name: total fat
components.evidence: Frying oil (corn/vegetable oil) dominates the lipid fraction.

components.name: oleic acid (C18:1)
components.source: USDA FDC 168127 total MUFA = 2.558 g/28.35 g
components.evidence: Approximated by total monounsaturated fatty acids (oleic ~all of MUFA).

components.name: Chloride
components.source: Counter-ion of added NaCl (~780 mg/100g by stoichiometry to Na)
components.evidence: Present as the counter-ion of table salt; not directly assayed.

components.name: Phosphate (as P)
components.evidence: Much bound as phytate; see phytic acid.

components.name: Calcium
components.evidence: Elevated by lime (Ca(OH)2) nixtamalization of the corn.

components.name: Molybdate (molybdenum)
components.source: Genuine ultratrace nutrient of cereals; not quantified in USDA FDC 168127
components.evidence: Retained as a trace essential; amount not measured for this food.

components.name: Fluoride
components.evidence: Trace, from process water; not growth-relevant.

components.name: Sulfate
components.evidence: Minor; retained as an ionic S source.

components.name: Ammonium
components.evidence: Retained as an inorganic N ion; not separately assayed.

components.name: Vitamin E (alpha-tocopherol)
components.evidence: Notably high (corn oil); corrects the unquantified current entry.

components.name: Vitamin K1 (phylloquinone)
components.evidence: Total vitamin K measured is 22.9 ug/100g; the balance is dihydrophylloquinone from the frying oil (see keep_uncovered).

components.name: Vitamin A (RAE)
components.evidence: Entirely from provitamin-A carotenoids (no preformed retinol).

components.name: L-ascorbate (vitamin C)
components.evidence: Not detected in the finished chip; retained as null-level.

components.name: Glycine betaine
components.source: USDA FDC 168127 betaine = 0.0 mg (present in cereals generally but not quantified here)
components.evidence: Retained; osmoprotectant, but reported as null/0 for this product.

remove.name: Cholesterol
remove.reason: Animal sterol; plain corn chips are plant-based and fried in vegetable/corn oil. USDA FDC 168127 cholesterol = 0 mg. FooDB predicted, biologically implausible.

remove.name: Lactose C12H22O11
remove.reason: Dairy disaccharide; no milk in plain corn chips. USDA lactose = 0.00 g. FooDB noise.

remove.name: D-Galactose
remove.reason: Lactose/dairy-derived monosaccharide; USDA galactose = 0.00 g. Not present in a corn chip. FooDB noise.

remove.name: Retinol
remove.reason: Preformed (animal) vitamin A; corn supplies only provitamin-A carotenoids. USDA retinol = 0 ug. Implausible in a plant food.

remove.name: Cyanocobalamin
remove.reason: Vitamin B12 is absent in unfortified plant foods; USDA B12 = 0 ug. FooDB predicted.

remove.name: Vitamin D
remove.reason: Absent in an unfortified plant corn snack; USDA vitamin D = 0 ug.

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: Free cobalt ion; non-nutritive trace element, not a growth nutrient of this food.

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: Non-nutritive trace contaminant, not a corn-chip growth nutrient.

remove.name: Hexanoate (n-C6:0)
remove.reason: USDA FDC 168127 = 0.000 g. Short-chain fatty acid characteristic of coconut/palm-kernel/dairy fat, not corn frying oil. FooDB noise.

remove.name: Dodecanoic acid
remove.reason: Lauric acid; USDA FDC 168127 = 0.000 g. Coconut/palm-kernel marker, absent in corn-oil chip. FooDB noise.

missing_added: starch quantified as the DOMINANT carbon source (~50.7 g/100g)
phytic acid (the one genuine organic acid, ~0.7 g/100g) - organic acids were otherwise essentially absent
L-phenylalanine and L-valine (both missing from the current amino-acid list)
corrected L-serine (current record had non-biological D-serine)
quantified individual fatty acids: linoleic (dominant), oleic, palmitic, stearic, alpha-linolenic
quantified full mineral panel (Na, K, Ca, P, Mg, Fe, Zn, Cu, Mn, Se)
quantified full vitamin panel with vitamin E corrected to ~7 mg/100g
keep_uncovered: 2',3'-Dihydro-phytomenadione / dihydrophylloquinone (genuine vitamin-K vitamer formed in hydrogenated frying oil; ~16.5 ug/100g by difference)
Lutein + zeaxanthin (~520 ug/100g; genuine maize xanthophylls, USDA FDC 168127)
sources: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, 'Snacks, plain, chips, extruded, corn-based', FDC ID 168127 (full nutrient panel; per-100g = per-oz values x 3.5273)
USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, 'Snacks, corn-based, extruded, chips, plain', mirrored via University of Rochester Medical Center encyclopedia 19003-1 (cross-check)
USDA FoodData Central, 'Cornmeal, yellow, enriched, degermed', FDC ID 168867 (maize amino-acid profile used as proxy, scaled to chip protein)
Urizar & Bressani 1997 phytic-acid-in-nixtamal data, reviewed in Felix-Samano et al., Int J Food Sci 2025, doi:10.1155/ijfo/6961009
notes: DOMINANT carbon/energy source is STARCH (~50.7 g/100g); free sugars are trivial (sucrose 0.28 g/100g, glucose/fructose/maltose ~0). Unlike fruits/dairy this food is UNFERMENTED, so classic organic acids (lactic, acetic, citric, malic, tartaric) are essentially absent - the only genuine organic acid is phytic acid (~0.7 g/100g), which also chelates the phosphate/mineral pool. Protein (~6.1 g/100g) is maize zein/glutelin (glutamate-, leucine-, proline-rich; lysine- and tryptophan-limiting; amino acids are a cornmeal-derived proxy). Lipid (~33 g/100g) is frying oil dominated by linoleic acid. Calcium is elevated by lime nixtamalization. Removed animal/dairy artifacts (cholesterol, lactose, galactose, retinol, B12, vitamin D) and non-nutritive trace ions (Co2+, Ni) plus zero-value MCFAs (C6:0, C12:0).
